commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Memory Recall guidance and deferred memory-tool loading instructions to native Codex app-server turns, updates Codex memory-routing docs, and expands Codex app-server regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +201, Tests +151, Docs +1. Total +353 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence current-main path: tool-routed Codex MEMORY.md only receives the workspace-memory pointer, while the PR body's devbox log shows the after-fix Memory Recall path and memory_search/memory_get calls.
commands.review_followup: - No repair lane is needed: I found no discrete patch defect for automation; this draft needs normal maintainer review and CI handling.

target.title: fix(codex): restore memory recall guidance
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore the memory-core recall instruction for Codex native app-server turns when workspace memory is routed through memory tools.
- Keep PR #87383's intended behavior: do not paste raw workspace `MEMORY.md` into normal Codex native turns when memory tools are available.
- Add regression coverage for `memory_search`/`memory_get`, deferred memory tools, and the tool-disabled fallback path.
- AI-assisted: yes. I understand what the code changes and validated it in the real OpenClaw path below.

## Linked context

Related: #87383

This fixes a regression from #87383. That PR correctly routed native Codex workspace `MEMORY.md` through memory tools to keep raw memory out of the prompt budget, but the Codex app-server path only left a memory-file pointer. It did not carry the memory-core `## Memory Recall` instruction into the native Codex collaboration/developer-instruction surface, so Codex could see/search memory tools without the normal obligation to use them before answering personalized questions.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex native turns in OpenClaw could receive searchable memory tools without the Memory Recall instruction, so personalized recommendations could skip saved user constraints until corrected later.
- Real environment tested: Devbox OpenClaw gateway from exact PR head `326a306ccb58b2e4f530c0fef9b84206aa19706c`, Telegram direct route, memory-core + codex + telegram plugins loaded from the PR checkout `dist`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the checkout with `pnpm build`, restarted the gateway service from `/home/rubencu/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-recall`, sent the same Telegram prompt, inspected the resulting runtime trajectory.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted runtime log and trace excerpt:

```text
2026-06-09T02:47:32Z [plugins] loading codex from /home/rubencu/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-recall/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
2026-06-09T02:47:34Z [plugins] loading memory-core from /home/rubencu/workplace/openclaw-codex-memory-recall/dist/extensions/memory-core/index.js
2026-06-09T02:47:35Z [telegram] [default] starting provider (@SalmeronDevBot)
2026-06-09T02:49:07Z telegram inbound: [redacted user/chat] "I am about to meet the SUB of the Amex Platinum. What card should I get next?"
2026-06-09T02:49:08Z session turn created: runId=208771d7-aed1-4df3-9e21-93dbefc1b845 sessionId=3f25567b-95a6-4575-a73b-9902a5329256

trajectory context.compiled:
  Deferred searchable OpenClaw dynamic tools available: ... memory_get, memory_search ...
  ## Codex Memory Tool Loading
  Memory tools memory_search or memory_get may be searchable rather than directly listed in native Codex turns...
  ## Memory Recall
  Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search...

trajectory tool.call line 4: memory_search args={"query":"[redacted user] credit cards Amex Platinum SUB Chase Sapphire Venture X travel points 5/24","maxResults":5,"corpus":"all"}
trajectory tool.result line 5: memory_search success=true, result path memory/2026-05-20.md, snippet includes "use known account-opening dates" and 5/24 context
trajectory tool.call line 6: memory_get args={"path":"memory/2026-05-20.md","from":1,"lines":25,"corpus":"memory"}
trajectory tool.result line 7: memory_get success=true, returned saved constraints including no business cards, Sapphire/5/24 caveats, and recent Amex Platinum context
trajectory message line 34: "Why: you're effectively out of Chase because of 5/24, and you've said no business cards..."
2026-06-09T02:52:58Z [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=[redacted] messageId=216
```

- Observed result after fix: The first answer used recalled memory before recommending a card: it treated Chase as off the board because of 5/24 and preserved the no-business-card constraint instead of asking/hedging about those known facts.
- What was not tested: Non-Codex harnesses were not live-tested; this proof covers the native Codex app-server path through Telegram on a devbox gateway, not every channel/provider combination.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Private Telegram ids, profile names, auth profile ids, and card inventory details are redacted. The proof uses the devbox OpenClaw gateway and copied runtime trajectory/log output.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): In the original reported behavior, Codex answered the same kind of personalized card question without first applying saved card-opening/5/24 memory, then corrected itself only after being asked what cards were already known. The code-level regression was introduced by #87383: native Codex stopped pasting raw `MEMORY.md` into ordinary tool-enabled turns and received only a memory-file pointer, not memory-core's Memory Recall instruction.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/token-use.md extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-test-harness.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts` — 96 tests passed
- `timeout 300 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts` — 24 tests passed
- `timeout 900 pnpm build`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `codex review --base origin/main` — no actionable correctness issues found

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

What is the highest-risk area? Prompt-surface behavior for native Codex memory routing.

How is that risk mitigated? The change reuses the memory-core prompt builder when available, preserves #87383's no-raw-`MEMORY.md` routing behavior, and adds focused coverage for searchable memory tools, deferred tool loading, and fallback memory injection.

## Current review state

Next action: draft PR opened for maintainer/author review.

Waiting on: PR CI and maintainer review.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: none yet.
target.author: rubencu
